question: Zanele had to classify Earth for homework 97. Which group of planets does it belong to?
correct_answer: The inner, rocky planets
wrong_answers_pool: The icy comets
The moons of Jupiter
The dwarf planets
The asteroid belt
The rings of Saturn
The exoplanets
explanation: The first four planets are smaller, solid, and rocky.

question: Kevin asked the teacher (Question 37): Why do we use aluminium foil for the inside of electrical wires and cotton string for the outside covering?
correct_answer: The aluminium foil allows electricity to flow, while the cotton string protects us from getting shocked.
wrong_answers_pool: The cotton string makes the wire heavier, and the aluminium foil makes it colourful.
The aluminium foil is cheaper than the cotton string.
Both the aluminium foil and cotton string are perfect insulators.
The aluminium foil creates electricity and the cotton string destroys it.
Both the aluminium foil and cotton string are excellent conductors.
The cotton string allows electricity to flow, while the aluminium foil stops it.
explanation: Wires have a conductive core to carry current and an insulating sheath for safety.

subtopic: Mains electricity (including fossil fuels, cost, and renewable energy)
question: In life skills lesson 66, Sipho learned that electricity is not free. How do households usually pay for mains electricity?
correct_answer: By buying prepaid electricity tokens or paying a monthly municipal bill based on usage.
wrong_answers_pool: By planting trees in the garden.
By returning the electricity they didn't use in a bucket.
It is completely free for everyone always.
By catching electricity in a jar.
By trading solar panels for it.
By paying once when they buy the house.
explanation: Consumers pay for electricity per kilowatt-hour used.

question: Sarah kept orange trees in complete darkness for 91 days. Why did they eventually stop growing?
correct_answer: They cannot perform photosynthesis without light energy.
wrong_answers_pool: They need light to produce nitrogen.
They cannot absorb water without light.
The darkness stops them from absorbing oxygen.
They cannot perform cellular respiration without light.
They will run out of carbon dioxide.
They will absorb too much carbon dioxide.
explanation: Without light energy from the sun, plants cannot make their own food through photosynthesis.

subtopic: Movements of the Earth (rotation and revolution)
question: For question 91, Lerato had to name the movement of the Earth in its path around the Sun. What is it?
correct_answer: Revolution
wrong_answers_pool: Rotation
Tidal pull
Twisting
Eclipsing
Hovering
Pulsating
explanation: Earth revolves (orbits) around the Sun.

question: Sipho replaced a switch with a steel paperclip in circuit 79. What will happen to the circuit?
correct_answer: The circuit will remain closed and electricity will flow because it is a conductor.
wrong_answers_pool: The electricity will stop completely.
The wires will instantly melt away.
The electricity will flow backwards into the battery.
The battery will immediately explode.
The circuit will be broken because it is an insulator.
The circuit will produce a loud buzzing sound only.
explanation: Because steel paperclip is a metal and a conductor, it completes the circuit just like a closed switch.

question: Nomsa noted in log 48 that sawdust did NOT form a solution when mixed with water. Why?
correct_answer: Because it is insoluble and does not dissolve in water.
wrong_answers_pool: Because it evaporates too fast.
Because it is lighter than air.
Because it is magnetic.
Because the water is too wet.
Because it turns the water into a solid.
Because it is a gas.
explanation: Insoluble substances like sawdust do not dissolve in water.

subtopic: Systems to solve problems using circuits
question: Before building the burglar alarm for assignment 13, Busi drew a circuit diagram using standard symbols. Why is this important?
correct_answer: It helps to plan and communicate exactly how components should be connected before building it.
wrong_answers_pool: It makes the components cheaper to buy.
It prevents the wires from getting tangled in the drawing.
It automatically builds the circuit for you.
It ensures the battery is fully charged.
It generates free electricity on the paper.
It makes the battery last longer in real life.
explanation: Circuit diagrams use universal symbols to clearly plan and communicate the design of an electrical system.

question: Lerato made a solution of jelly powder and water (Mix 34). What do we call the water?
correct_answer: The solvent
wrong_answers_pool: The crystal
The mixture
The solute
The precipitate
The filter
The residue
explanation: The liquid in which a solute dissolves is called the solvent.

question: Johan had to classify Uranus for homework 33. Which group of planets does it belong to?
correct_answer: The outer, gas giants
wrong_answers_pool: The solar flares
The terrestrial planets
The dwarf planets
The asteroids
The inner, rocky planets
The meteoroids
explanation: The outer four planets are massive and made mostly of gas.

question: In study 61, Lerato researched dung beetles. What is their role in an ecosystem?
correct_answer: They break down dead plants and animals and return nutrients to the soil.
wrong_answers_pool: They hunt and eat primary consumers.
They provide energy directly to the sun.
They produce food using sunlight.
They consume living plants only.
They act as the top predators.
They absorb carbon dioxide from the air.
explanation: Decomposers recycle nutrients by breaking down dead organic matter.

question: For test 41, Johan has a circuit with a battery, wires, and a cell (battery). What happens if a wire is cut?
correct_answer: The circuit becomes open (broken) and the cell (battery) will stop working.
wrong_answers_pool: The cut wire will produce a bright light.
The battery will start spinning.
The electricity will find a new path through the air.
The battery will recharge itself instantly.
The cell (battery) will absorb energy from the air.
The circuit remains closed.
explanation: Cutting a wire creates an open circuit, preventing electricity from flowing.

question: Johan was asked in exam 19: Why must an electric circuit form a continuous, unbroken loop from the positive to the negative terminal of the battery?
correct_answer: Because electrical current can only flow if there is a complete pathway.
wrong_answers_pool: Because light bulbs need oxygen to burn.
Because the wires will melt otherwise.
Because insulators need a gap to work.
Because the negative terminal pushes the positive terminal away.
Because switches only work in broken circuits.
Because batteries only work in the dark.
explanation: A complete, closed loop is strictly required for electrical current to flow.

question: Zanele made a solution of coffee powder and water (Mix 56). What do we call the water?
correct_answer: The solvent
wrong_answers_pool: The precipitate
The residue
The suspension
The filter
The mixture
The crystal
explanation: The liquid in which a solute dissolves is called the solvent.

question: Nomsa studied how acid mine drainage entering a river affects the natural water mixture (Case 64). What was the conclusion?
correct_answer: It introduces harmful substances that make the water unsafe for drinking and living organisms.
wrong_answers_pool: It turns the water into a safe solution of minerals.
It cools the water down to safe temperatures.
It purifies the water naturally.
It turns the liquid water into a gas.
It makes the river flow faster and cleaner.
It only changes the colour but is perfectly safe.
explanation: Pollutants degrade water quality, making it a hazardous mixture.

subtopic: Systems to explore the Moon and Mars (rovers)
question: Zanele researched for paper 84: How do scientists on Earth control a space probe that is millions of kilometers away on Mars?
correct_answer: By sending complex computer commands via radio signals, which take several minutes to travel there.
wrong_answers_pool: The robot is entirely controlled by aliens.
Using a very long electrical wire.
By shouting through a giant megaphone.
Using invisible laser strings.
By waiting for the robot to come back and get instructions.
By controlling it in real-time with a joystick like a video game.
explanation: Due to the vast distance, radio signals take time to travel between Earth and Mars, so rovers execute programmed sequences.

question: For essay 95, Kevin explained why Neptune stays in its orbit around the Sun instead of flying off into space. What is the reason?
correct_answer: The strong force of gravity from the Sun pulls it inward, keeping it in orbit.
wrong_answers_pool: It is magnetic and sticks to the Sun.
It is stuck in a giant cloud of gas.
It is pushed by the solar wind.
It has its own engine.
It is attached by an invisible string.
It bounces off the asteroid belt.
explanation: Gravity is the attractive force that keeps celestial bodies in their orbits.

question: Zanele made a solution of Epsom salts and water (Mix 62). What do we call the Epsom salts?
correct_answer: The solute
wrong_answers_pool: The residue
The liquid
The insoluble solid
The solution
The solvent
The filter
explanation: The solid substance that dissolves is called the solute.

question: Sipho kept tomato plants in complete darkness for 86 days. Why did they eventually stop growing?
correct_answer: They cannot perform photosynthesis without light energy.
wrong_answers_pool: They will run out of carbon dioxide.
They will absorb too much carbon dioxide.
They need light to produce nitrogen.
They cannot perform cellular respiration without light.
They cannot absorb water without light.
Their roots will rot in the dark.
explanation: Without light energy from the sun, plants cannot make their own food through photosynthesis.

question: In biology test 33, Piet was asked: In which part of the potato plants does photosynthesis mainly take place?
correct_answer: The leaves
wrong_answers_pool: The roots
The fruit
The flowers
The bark
The stem
The branches
explanation: Photosynthesis primarily takes place in the leaves of plants, where chlorophyll is found.

question: If Nomsa's meal 38 consists only of rice and beans, which major nutrient group is missing for a balanced diet?
correct_answer: Vitamins and minerals (fruits and vegetables)
wrong_answers_pool: Carbohydrates
Starches
Water
Proteins
Fiber only
Fats only
explanation: While rice provides carbs and beans provides protein, vitamins and minerals from fruits and vegetables are missing.

question: Jabu drew a desert food web (Diagram 85). What does the arrow pointing from the springboks to the spiders mean?
correct_answer: It shows the flow of energy from the springboks to the spiders.
wrong_answers_pool: It shows that they are friends.
It means they live in the same shelter.
It means they compete for water.
It indicates that both are decomposers.
It shows the {prim} is a producer.
It shows the flow of water.
explanation: Arrows in a food web show the direction of energy flow (who is eaten by whom).

subtopic: Movements of the Moon (rotation and revolution)
question: In chart 56, Sarah tracked the Moon. How long does it take for the Moon to revolve once around the Earth?
correct_answer: About 27 to 29 days (roughly a month)
wrong_answers_pool: 50 days
365 days
24 hours
7 days
10 years
12 hours
explanation: It takes roughly a month for the Moon to complete its orbit around Earth.

subtopic: Systems to solve problems using circuits

question: Nomsa kept bean plants in complete darkness for 50 days. Why did they eventually stop growing?

The darkness stops them from absorbing oxygen.

question: Lerato explained how a large optical telescope like optical telescope works to see stars (Presentation 99). What is the correct method?
correct_answer: It uses curved mirrors or lenses to gather and focus light from distant objects.
wrong_answers_pool: It shoots a laser beam to illuminate the stars.
It measures the wind speed in space.
It sends out radio waves and waits for an echo.
It captures sound waves from space.
It uses magnets to pull the image closer.
It relies on x-rays from the sun.
explanation: Optical telescopes gather visible light using precision optics.

question: Sarah asked the teacher (Question 98): Why do we use iron nail for the inside of electrical wires and wooden stick for the outside covering?
correct_answer: The iron nail allows electricity to flow, while the wooden stick protects us from getting shocked.
wrong_answers_pool: The iron nail creates electricity and the wooden stick destroys it.
The wooden stick makes the electricity flow faster than the iron nail.

The wooden stick makes the wire heavier, and the iron nail makes it colourful.

question: For test 63, Piet has a circuit with a battery, wires, and a switch. What happens if a wire is cut?
correct_answer: The circuit becomes open (broken) and the switch will stop working.
wrong_answers_pool: The cut wire will produce a bright light.
The switch will absorb energy from the air.
The circuit remains closed.

question: Busi explained how a large optical telescope like optical telescope works to see stars (Presentation 92). What is the correct method?
correct_answer: It uses curved mirrors or lenses to gather and focus light from distant objects.
wrong_answers_pool: It relies on x-rays from the sun.

explanation: Optical telescopes gather visible light using precision optics.

question: Research project 55: Jabu investigated how eating too much processed food high in sugar and unhealthy fats affects the body over time. What did they find?
correct_answer: It can lead to obesity, tooth decay, and heart problems.
wrong_answers_pool: It improves eyesight and brain function.
It increases the amount of oxygen in the blood.
It prevents all types of cancers.
It helps muscles repair faster after exercise.
It makes the bones much stronger.
It cures diseases like scurvy and rickets.
explanation: A poor diet high in sugar and unhealthy fats is linked to various health issues like obesity.

question: Sarah had to classify Venus for homework 9. Which group of planets does it belong to?
correct_answer: The inner, rocky planets
wrong_answers_pool: The outer, gas giants
The moons of Jupiter
The exoplanets
The rings of Saturn
The icy comets
The dwarf planets
explanation: The first four planets are smaller, solid, and rocky.

subtopic: Systems to explore the Moon and Mars (rovers)
question: In discussion 34, Nomsa debated: Why do scientists send robotic machines like a Mars rover to Mars instead of humans right now?
correct_answer: It is safer, much cheaper, and machines do not need food, water, or oxygen.
wrong_answers_pool: Because humans would melt instantly.
Because humans are too heavy for rockets.
Because robots can breathe carbon dioxide.
Because robots are faster than light.
Because Mars has no ground to stand on.
Because robots can live forever without power.
explanation: Uncrewed missions avoid the extreme risks and life-support costs of human spaceflight.

question: For test 79, Piet has a circuit with a battery, wires, and a switch. What happens if a wire is cut?
correct_answer: The circuit becomes open (broken) and the switch will stop working.
wrong_answers_pool: The circuit remains closed.
The battery will start spinning.
The battery will recharge itself instantly.
The electricity will find a new path through the air.
The switch will absorb energy from the air.
The cut wire will produce a bright light.
explanation: Cutting a wire creates an open circuit, preventing electricity from flowing.

subtopic: Movements of the Moon (rotation and revolution)
question: Busi wondered in entry 3: Why do we always see the same side of the Moon from Earth?
correct_answer: Because the Moon's rotation time is exactly the same as its revolution time around Earth.
wrong_answers_pool: Because clouds always cover the other side.
Because the Moon is flat.
Because the Earth stops the Moon from spinning.
Because the dark side of the Moon has no light.
Because the Moon does not rotate at all.
Because the Earth is spinning too fast.
explanation: Tidal locking means the Moon spins on its axis at the same rate it orbits Earth.

question: Johan replaced a switch with a brass key in circuit 30. What will happen to the circuit?
correct_answer: The circuit will remain closed and electricity will flow because it is a conductor.
wrong_answers_pool: The electricity will stop completely.
The wires will instantly melt away.
The circuit will produce a loud buzzing sound only.
The circuit will be broken because it is an insulator.
The battery will immediately explode.
The light bulb will turn into a motor.
explanation: Because brass key is a metal and a conductor, it completes the circuit just like a closed switch.

question: Sarah needs to make jelly powder dissolve faster in water for test 85. Which action should they take?
correct_answer: Using hot water and stirring.
wrong_answers_pool: Blowing air into the water.
Using less water and freezing it.
Putting it in a dark cupboard.
Covering the glass with a cloth.
Adding more solid and not stirring.
Adding oil to the water.
explanation: Increasing the temperature and stirring increases the rate of dissolving.

question: Johan needs to make salt dissolve faster in water for test 23. Which action should they take?
correct_answer: Using hot water and stirring.
wrong_answers_pool: Using less water and freezing it.
Adding oil to the water.
Putting it in a dark cupboard.
Using ice cold water and leaving it still.
Adding more solid and not stirring.
Blowing air into the water.
explanation: Increasing the temperature and stirring increases the rate of dissolving.

subtopic: Movements of the Earth (rotation and revolution)
question: In project 53, Piet described the Earth's full revolution around the Sun. How long does it take, and what does this movement, combined with its tilted axis, cause?
correct_answer: It takes 365 and a quarter days (1 year) and causes the seasons.
wrong_answers_pool: It takes 7 days and causes the tides.
It takes 24 hours and causes day and night.
It takes 10 years and causes climate change.
It takes 30 days and causes the phases of the Moon.
It takes 1 year and causes eclipses only.
It takes 24 hours and causes the seasons.
explanation: The yearly orbit combined with the tilt of the Earth's axis causes seasons.

question: Zanele explained how a large optical telescope like SALT (Southern African Large Telescope) works to see stars (Presentation 21). What is the correct method?
correct_answer: It uses curved mirrors or lenses to gather and focus light from distant objects.
wrong_answers_pool: It uses a giant magnifying glass only.
It measures the wind speed in space.
It uses magnets to pull the image closer.
It sends out radio waves and waits for an echo.
It relies on x-rays from the sun.
It shoots a laser beam to illuminate the stars.
explanation: Optical telescopes gather visible light using precision optics.

subtopic: Movements of the Moon (rotation and revolution)
question: Jabu wondered in entry 66: Why do we always see the same side of the Moon from Earth?
correct_answer: Because the Moon's rotation time is exactly the same as its revolution time around Earth.
wrong_answers_pool: Because the Earth stops the Moon from spinning.
Because the Sun's gravity locks it in place.
Because the dark side of the Moon has no light.
Because clouds always cover the other side.
Because the Moon is flat.
Because the Moon does not rotate at all.
explanation: Tidal locking means the Moon spins on its axis at the same rate it orbits Earth.

question: Piet asked in question 76: Why is dietary fibre important in our daily nutrition, even though it does not provide energy?
correct_answer: It helps to move food through the digestive system and prevents constipation.
wrong_answers_pool: It builds new skin cells.
It builds strong muscles and bones.
It fights off viral infections directly.
It helps red blood cells carry oxygen.
It forms the structure of our teeth.
It provides a concentrated source of energy.
explanation: Fibre is crucial for healthy digestion and bowel movements.

question: Thabo had to draw the particles of helium for question 14. How should they be arranged?
correct_answer: They are very far apart and move freely in all directions.
wrong_answers_pool: They are linked together like a chain.
They only move downwards.
They are packed tightly in a neat row.
They form a rigid square structure.
They vibrate in fixed positions.
They are close together but slide past each other.
explanation: Gas particles have a lot of energy, are spaced far apart, and move rapidly in all directions.
